economic_notes: Subsidiaries include Widjikiwe Holdings Corp (WHC), Widjikiwe L.P., TFN Construction L.P. (TFNCLP), and Widjikiwe Gas Bar L.P. (WGB). WGB not properly accounted for under equity method. WGB has supply contracts with exclusive gasoline supplier (10-year contract expiring Nov 2030) and general merchandise supplier (7-year contract expiring Nov 2027). New policing infrastructure funding agreement signed March 2022 with federal contribution up to $3,026,400. Community hall/youth center construction project underway.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED because Widjikiwe Gas Bar LP was not accounted for using the modified equity method as required under PSAS — the entity's data was unavailable. This affects surplus and financial assets for both 2022 and 2021 by an undetermined amount. Nation is located in Quebec. Three subsidiaries consolidated: Widjikiwe Holdings Corp, Widjikiwe L.P., and TFN Construction L.P. Widjikiwe Gas Bar L.P. is a GBE that should be equity-accounted but was not. Current liabilities calculated as accounts payable $2,416,904 + due to ISC $226,945 + due to Widjikiwe Gas Bar $156,657 + current portion of long-term debt $344,179 = $3,144,685. Total government transfers calculated from segment disclosure: federal $35,044,912 + provincial $958,021 = $36,002,933. ISC funding = Fixed + Block + Flexible + Set contributions = $33,745,046. OSR = total revenue $25,994,260 minus total government transfers recognized in revenue (adjusted for deferred revenue movements — used segment federal $35,044,912 + provincial $958,021 less deferred revenue adjustments; alternatively OSR approximated as total revenue minus ISC and other identified government transfers). Pension plan issue discovered post year-end re compulsory nature of RBA plan — amount unquantifiable, no provision recorded. Canada Emergency Wage Subsidy received by TFNCLP offset against salaries.
